# zabbix_template_import (Action)

Imports Zabbix templates from an export file (XML, YAML, or JSON) via the `configuration.import` API. This is a stateless action: it provides no drift detection and no cleanup — imported templates remain in Zabbix after `terraform destroy`. Reference imported templates via the `zabbix_template` data source.

The `source` attribute accepts raw content; compose it with the `file()` function or the `http` data source.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Standalone invocation — import templates from a local XML export file.
# Run with: terraform apply -invoke=action.zabbix_template_import.example
action "zabbix_template_import" "example" {
  config {
    source = file("templates.xml")
    format = "xml"
  }
}

# Triggered invocation — import templates whenever a template group is created.
resource "zabbix_template_group" "monitoring" {
  name = "Monitoring"

  lifecycle {
    action_trigger {
      events  = [after_create]
      actions = [action.zabbix_template_import.on_create]
    }
  }
}

action "zabbix_template_import" "on_create" {
  config {
    source = file("templates.xml")
    format = "xml"
  }
}
```

<!-- action schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `format` (String) Format of the export content. Must match the actual content format. Accepted values: `xml`, `yaml`, `json`.
- `source` (String) Raw XML, YAML, or JSON content of a Zabbix template export. Use `file("path/to/export.xml")` or the `http` data source to supply the content.

### Optional

- `rules` (Attributes) Import rules controlling which entities are created, updated, or deleted. Omit to use the defaults: `create_missing=true`, `update_existing=true`, `delete_missing=false` for all entities. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules))

<a id="nestedatt--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules`

Optional:

- `discovery_rules` (Attributes) Rules for low-level discovery rule objects. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--discovery_rules))
- `graphs` (Attributes) Rules for graph objects. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--graphs))
- `http_tests` (Attributes) Rules for HTTP test (web scenario) objects. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--http_tests))
- `items` (Attributes) Rules for item objects. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--items))
- `template_dashboards` (Attributes) Rules for template dashboard objects. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--template_dashboards))
- `template_groups` (Attributes) Rules for template group objects. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--template_groups))
- `template_linkage` (Attributes) Rules for template linkage. `delete_missing=true` unlinks parent templates that are absent in the import source, without deleting inherited entities. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--template_linkage))
- `templates` (Attributes) Rules for template objects. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--templates))
- `triggers` (Attributes) Rules for trigger objects. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--triggers))
- `value_maps` (Attributes) Rules for value map objects. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--value_maps))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--discovery_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.discovery_rules`

Optional:

- `create_missing` (Boolean)
- `delete_missing` (Boolean)
- `update_existing` (Boolean)
